item.title: 2 people climb to top of NYC's Empire State Building, appear to get engaged
item.description: Two people climbed to the top of New York City's Empire State Building, unfurled a banner, and then apparently got engaged Wednesday afternoon.

item.title: Trump lauds "very good meetings" as indirect U.S.-Iran talks resume
item.description: Vance says the U.S. has accomplished its "core mission" in Iran, as Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner meet mediators in Qatar, but not Iranians.

item.title: Should you stop contributing to retirement while paying off debt?
item.description: Paying down debt matters, but pausing retirement contributions to do so may not be the smartest financial move.

item.title: Trump lands in North Dakota after first flight on new Air Force One gifted by Qatar
item.description: President Trump landed in North Dakota after taking his maiden voyage on the new Air Force One plane donated by Qatar. CBS News' Nancy Cordes has more.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding events set to begin in NYC, sources say
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding events are set to begin in New York City, sources say. CBS News' Jo Ling Kent has the latest.

item.title: Sophia Bush and Jerry O'Connell on having fun while filming their new comedy "Summer's Last Resort"
item.description: Sophia Bush and Jerry O'Connell join CBS News 24/7 Mornings to discuss their new comedy, "Summer's Last Resort," which premieres on Tubi July 3.

item.title: Trump optimistic about indirect talks with Iran as Tehran vows to respond to any threats
item.description: President Trump expressed optimism about the U.S. and Iran's indirect talks in Doha, Qatar, while Tehran vowed to respond to any new threats. Eric Lob, an associate professor of politics and international relations at Florida International University, joins with more.

item.title: Trump takes first trip on new Air Force One gifted by Qatar
item.description: President Trump is taking his first trip on his new Air Force One, a luxury plane gifted by Qatar. CBS News' Natalie Brand has the latest.

item.title: 2 people climb to top of Empire State Building and fly banner
item.description: Two people are at the top of the Empire State Building with a banner that reads "When the power of love beats the love of power, the world will know peace." CBS News New York reports.

item.title: What to know about Medicare covering GLP-1 drugs
item.description: Medicare starts covering GLP-1 drugs on July 1. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

item.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

item.title: Justice Department sues Virginia, California over gun laws
item.description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche said "the Constitution is not a suggestion" and that "the Second Amendment is a sacred right belonging to all Americans."

item.title: This July 4th could be hottest ever in parts of U.S.
item.description: Extremely dangerous heat, coupled with humidity, could result in heat index readings of 100 to 115 degrees from the Midwest to the East Coast, forecasters said.

item.title: Record-high temperatures expected in cities across U.S. through Fourth of July
item.description: More than two dozen states are under heat alerts, impacting around 200 million people across the central and eastern U.S. Dozens of cities are expected to endure record highs through the Fourth of July. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: Harvard professor known for alien theories to lead White House UFO council
item.description: Avi Loeb, a Harvard astronomer who has made headlines with his theories about alien encounters, will lead a White House scientific advisory council on UFOs.

item.title: Sophia Bush and Jerry O'Connell talk new comedy "Summer's Last Resort"
item.description: Sophia Bush and Jerry O'Connell talk to "CBS Mornings" about starring in the coming-of-age comedy "Summer's Last Resort." Bush discusses what drew her to the project and says there was "never a dull day on set," while O'Connell reveals what his daughters thought about the film.

item.title: Future impact of Supreme Court's key rulings
item.description: The Supreme Court's most recent term ended with some landmark decisions on birthright citizenship and transgender athletes' ability to compete. CBS News' Jan Crawford breaks down the major cases.

item.title: Trump administration removed AI guardrails on powerful Claude models, Anthropic says
item.description: Tech giant Anthropic says that the federal government has now removed restrictions from its robust Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models. Axios reporter Madison Mills has more on artificial intelligence regulation.

item.title: Can your spouse inherit your credit card debt?
item.description: Your spouse isn't automatically responsible for your credit card debt after you die, but there are some exceptions.

item.title: U.S. prepares to take on Bosnia and Herzegovina in World Cup knockout stage
item.description: The U.S. on Wednesday will open the World Cup knockout stage against Bosnia and Herzegovina, hoping to punch their ticket to the round of 16. Meanwhile, some of the sport's biggest names led their teams to victory on Tuesday. Nicole Valdes has more.

item.title: Sony to stop making physical discs for PlayStation starting in 2028
item.description: After January 2028, new games will be available exclusively in digital format from the PlayStation Store and at retailers, Sony said.

item.title: Dog pulled from rubble 5 days after Venezuela quakes, video shows
item.description: Dramatic video showed the moment a rescue team pulled a small dog from the rubble nearly a week after twin earthquakes​ devastated Venezuela.

item.title: Actor Danny Glover reveals Alzheimer's diagnosis
item.description: Actor Danny Glover spoke about his Alzheimer's diagnosis in an interview with NBC's "Today" show, revealing that he has been living with the disease for several years.

item.title: Colorado's Melat Kiros poised to become first Gen Z member of Congress after primary win
item.description: Melat Kiros, the Democratic socialist candidate who won the primary for Colorado's 1st Congressional District, could become the first Gen Z member of Congress. CBS News' Taurean Small has the latest.

item.title: Trump takes first trip on Qatari-gifted Air Force One
item.description: President Trump is traveling Wednesday to North Dakota in what marks his first trip aboard the new Air Force One, which was gifted to the U.S. by the Qatari government last year.

item.title: Trump made over $1 billion on crypto ventures last year, disclosure shows
item.description: President Trump earned more than a billion dollars from crypto-related ventures alone last year, according to a financial disclosure, including from his meme coin business and his family's cryptocurrency firm.

item.title: New disease threats follow Trump administration's health program cuts
item.description: From screwworm to flesh-eating bacteria, mounting public health risks are emerging in the wake of deep cuts to federal health agencies and programs.

item.title: Anthropic says Trump admin. lifted restrictions on powerful Claude AI models
item.description: Artificial intelligence giant Anthropic says the federal government lifted a set of restrictions on its powerful Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, resolving a weekslong dispute.

item.title: Here's what to know about Medicare's new GLP-1 weight-loss drug program
item.description: For the first time, Medicare will cover GLP-1 drugs prescribed solely for weight loss for eligible beneficiaries at a $50 monthly copay.

item.title: Videos show U.S. rescue team pull mother, baby from rubble in Venezuela
item.description: Hundreds of U.S. search and rescue workers are on the ground in Venezuela after deadly quakes struck the South American nation.
